meta.title: Niela — Personalized meditation
meta.description: AI-generated meditation sessions, adapted to your spiritual tradition and how you feel today.

hero.subtitle: The app that respects your spiritual tradition — religious, spiritual, or secular.
hero.footnote: No spam. We'll let you know when we launch.

howItWorks.title: Five screens. One practice that adapts to you.
howItWorks.card1Text: 7 spiritual traditions. Set up your path: Zen, Tibetan, Andean, Sufi, Contemplative Christian, Islamic, or Secular.
howItWorks.card2Text: Tell us how you feel. Niela AI creates a unique session, adapted to your tradition and your day.
howItWorks.card3Text: 20 lessons per tradition. Theory, practice, and guided reflection. A complete path.
howItWorks.card4Text: Save your favorite meditations. Tags by emotional state. Come back to the ones that helped most.
howItWorks.card5Text: Track how you feel. Niela learns from your journey to personalize each session better.

howItWorks2.title: Simple. No shortcuts.
howItWorks2.step1Desc: Free on Google Play, no credit card required.
howItWorks2.step2Desc: Zen, Sufi, Christian, secular… or several at once.
howItWorks2.step3Desc: One lesson per day. No shortcuts. Like real zen.

traditions2.title: Your tradition, your path.
traditions2.subtitle: We're starting with the complete Zen course in Spanish. The other traditions arrive in the coming weeks.

roadmap.title: What's coming in Niela
col2Items: AI meditations based on your state
roadmap.emailCta: Leave your email and we'll notify you when what you're waiting for arrives:

whyExist.text: The big meditation apps are designed for one profile: Western, secular, English-speaking. Niela was born for everyone else — for those who meditate in Arabic, pray in Swahili, practice zazen in Buenos Aires, or contemplate in silence in Mexico City.
whyExist.title:
Meditation is universal.
But apps aren't.

comparison.title: Why Niela is different
comparison.footer: Comparison based on public information as of May 18, 2026. Headspace Ebb (AI companion) available only in English according to official Headspace Health announcement, October 2024.

founder.title: Who makes Niela
founder.text1: Hi, I'm Yunior Rivera. I'm building Niela from Italy, alone, with the help of practitioners from each tradition who validate the content.
founder.text2: No investors. No big team. Just deep respect for contemplative traditions and a drive to build something that truly helps you.
founder.text3: If you find bugs, have suggestions, or want to collaborate on a tradition's content — write to me directly.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.a1: Niela respects religious and spiritual traditions, and also includes a 100% secular option for those who prefer mental wellbeing without a religious component.
faq.q2: Do I need to believe in something?
faq.a2: No. Zen doesn't require religious belief. The other traditions are optional depending on your personal path.
faq.a3: The complete Zen course is free. In the future, personalized AI meditations will be part of an optional plan.
faq.a4: We don't sell information to third parties. We don't use Google Analytics or Meta Pixel. We comply with GDPR.
faq.q5: How do I delete my account?
faq.a5: From the app: Profile → Delete account. Everything is permanently deleted.
faq.q6: When does the iOS version arrive?
faq.a6: In the next 4 weeks. If you leave your email, we'll notify you when it's available.
faq.q7: When do the other traditions arrive?
faq.a7: We're starting this week with Zen. We'll launch one new tradition per month approximately. We'll notify you by email if you're interested in a specific one.
